# KL policy circuits: a short generator, not a succinct policy

2026-07-21. Status: **uniform construction + pinned exact finite
compression/rank diagnostics + floating Perron comparison**. Run

```bash
python3 experiments/kl/diagnose_policy_circuit_complexity.py
```

The answer depends sharply on what “low-complexity circuit” means.

* **Yes, weakly:** the level-`k` KL operator is generated by one uniform
  arithmetic/min wiring rule, with size linear in its `3^(k-1)`-coordinate
  output. Its `t`th iterate is a uniform circuit of size
  `O(t 3^(k-1))` and depth `O(t)`.
* **No evidence, strongly:** the available selected argmin words do not have a
  small digit automaton, ordered decision diagram, aligned recursive grammar,
  sparse low-degree `F_3` polynomial, or low-rank ternary tensor-train
  representation. Several of these failures are exact finite lower bounds in
  their stated models.
* **Unknown for general circuits:** none of these diagnostics rules out a
  `poly(k)`-size arithmetic/Boolean circuit which takes the ternary digits of a
  coordinate and returns its value or active label. Proving such a lower bound
  for unrestricted circuits would be far beyond the evidence here.

This is compatible with the same-policy defect-automaton no-go. A short
algorithm may generate a very complicated word, while deterministic
bisimulation of the word's transition system still has essentially full
state. Conversely, quotient explosion is not a general circuit lower bound.

## 1. The exact uniform circuit that does exist

Let `N=3^(k-1)` and index a KL coordinate by `i`, representing the residue
`m=3i+2 (mod 3^k)`. The transport source is

```text
t(i)=4i+2 mod N.                                      (1.1)
```

Rows `i=0 mod 3` are type two, rows `i=2 mod 3` are type eight, and rows
`i=1 mod 3` are neutral. Put `n=N/3`. Their branch parents are

```text
b_2(i)=4(i/3) mod n,
b_8(i)=2((i-2)/3)+1 mod n.                            (1.2)
```

The branch fiber over `b` is `(b,b+n,b+2n)`. Thus every output coordinate of
the strict operator is computed from one transport input and, on two thirds
of the rows, one three-input minimum. The full map has `O(N)` scalar,
addition, and minimum gates at constant depth; its wiring is computed from
`(k,i)` by elementary modular arithmetic on `O(k)`-bit indices. Replacing the
minimum by a power mean gives the same construction with one analytic
three-input gate.

This is more accurately an **arithmetic/min circuit** than a pure min-plus
circuit. In positive coordinates the transport and branch contributions are
added ordinarily. In logarithmic coordinates that outer addition becomes a
log-sum-exp, not a min-plus operation.

Starting from the all-ones vector and composing the map `t` times produces a
short uniform program and an output-linear circuit:

```text
description length: O(log k+log t),
global circuit size: O(t 3^(k-1)),
global circuit depth: O(t).                           (1.3)
```

This is a low Kolmogorov-description statement, not a succinct coordinate
formula. If one unfolds only a single output without sharing, its dependency
tree has up to four children per layer. If one shares equal `(time,state)`
nodes, its width can still become the whole level.

The checker makes that last statement concrete at `k=12`. From either
coordinate `0` or the coordinate representing `-1`, the full arithmetic/min
dependency cone reaches all `177,147` coordinates after exactly eleven
layers. If the selected policy is supplied in advance, so only the winning
branch child is retained, the corresponding cones still reach all coordinates
after respectively `84` and `82` layers. These are exact graph computations
on the pinned policy, not an all-level theorem.

A fixed strict policy `sigma` similarly gives a uniformly sparse linear
matrix `A_sigma`. The transport cycle makes it irreducible, so its Perron
problem is global even though each row is local. The unresolved compression
problem has merely moved into the lookup `u -> sigma(u)`.

At finite temperature, a winner label is only a shadow of the actual policy.
The exact dual form in `softmin-replica.md` assigns each branch fiber a
continuous tangent coefficient triple

```text
a_i=(1/3) M_p(z)^(1-p) z_i^(p-1).
```

Thus the softened operator is not linearized by storing one active trit. A
succinct soft policy would have to generate these coefficient triples, or the
Perron vector from which they are locally computed. Section 5 tests both the
coarser winner word and tensor ranks of the vector itself.

## 2. What was tested

For the selected feasible record `C_k`, define

```text
sigma_k(u)=argmin_(a in {0,1,2}) C_k(u+a 3^(k-2)).    (2.1)
```

Every minimum is strict for `k=12,...,15`. These are SHA-pinned exact integer
records, but they are **feasible subeigenvectors, not exact critical
eigenvectors**. The distinction matters: the exact results below describe the
record policies. Section 5 separately compares freshly iterated strict and
soft Perron vectors in floating point.

Four representation classes were tested.

1. **Ordered ternary branching programs / digit DFAs.** After a set `S` of
   digits has been read, two prefixes can share a state only if they induce the
   same residual function on every unread suffix. Counting distinct residual
   functions is therefore an exact width lower bound. It applies to any
   deterministic read-once digit order, but not to a branching program which
   rereads digits or to an unrestricted circuit.
2. **Aligned ternary subtree DAGs.** Recursively split the policy word into
   three equal blocks and merge identical subtrees. This is an exact reduced
   ordered decision diagram and an aligned straight-line grammar. Generic
   nonaligned grammars are only probed by ordinary compression.
3. **Tensor trains / weighted linear digit machines.** Matricize the exact
   coordinate tensor, or the one-hot policy tensor, across a ternary digit
   cut. Rank modulo the prime `65,521` is an exact lower bound for its
   characteristic-zero rank and hence for exact tensor-train bond dimension
   across that cut. This does not bound nonlinear arithmetic circuits.
4. **Canonical `F_3` algebraic normal form.** Every map
   `F_3^10 -> F_3` has a unique reduced polynomial with each variable exponent
   at most two. Exact interpolation measures sparsity and degree. A dense ANF
   can still have a short factored arithmetic circuit, so this is a diagnostic,
   not a general algebraic-circuit lower bound.

## 3. Exact policy-compression results

The one-symbol entropies are already essentially maximal
(`log_2 3=1.5849625...`). Packing four trits per byte and then applying ordinary
compression uses about `1.60` bits per trit, near the incompressible ternary
baseline.

| `k` | policy length | `H_1` | zlib / bz2 bits per trit | aligned DAG nodes, MSD / LSD | aligned peak, MSD / LSD |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 12 | 59,049 | 1.584844 | 1.60626 / 1.61696 | 8,878 / 8,579 | 5,568 / 5,269 |
| 13 | 177,147 | 1.584942 | 1.60775 / 1.60558 | 22,262 / 21,546 | 12,391 / 11,676 |
| 14 | 531,441 | 1.584962 | 1.60721 / 1.60281 | 48,213 / 47,638 | 19,683 / 19,679 |
| 15 | 1,594,323 | 1.584961 | 1.60775 / 1.60234 | 108,283 / 108,251 | 59,049 / 59,036 |

The DAG is smaller than the literal word because all three-output suffix
functions come from a set of only `27`, and analogous accidental merging
occurs near the leaves. Its size nevertheless grows exponentially. This is
not a bounded-state or `poly(k)` representation.

The arbitrary-order test is sharper. Every digit order has a prefix leaving
three digits unread. Exhausting every possible such prefix set gives:

| `k` | prefix digits read | subsets exhausted | minimum distinct residuals | policy length / 27 |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 12 | 7 | 120 | 2,187 | 2,187 |
| 13 | 8 | 165 | 6,553 | 6,561 |
| 14 | 9 | 220 | 19,668 | 19,683 |
| 15 | 10 | 286 | 58,945 | 59,049 |

Thus every ordered read-once ternary branching program for each displayed
word has width nearly one twenty-seventh of the whole truth table. At `k=12`,
exhaustive dynamic programming over all `2^10` digit subsets goes further: the
best possible digit ordering has total layered state count `8,579` and peak
width `5,269`. Least-significant-digit first attains both optima.

The exact canonical polynomial gives the same negative signal in a different
language. At `k=12`, `39,296` of `59,049` possible reduced `F_3` monomials are
nonzero (`0.66548`, essentially the random density `2/3`). The maximum total
degree is `19` out of `20`, and a nonzero coefficient uses all ten variables.
So neither bounded degree nor sparse ANF is latent in this policy.

## 4. Exact rank and cross-level tests

The following ranks are computed modulo `65,521`; therefore each is a rigorous
lower bound for rank over the rationals. `C` is the coordinate-value tensor.
For the policy, the output label is one-hot encoded as an extra ternary mode.

| `k` | value matricization | exact modular rank | policy matricization | exact modular rank |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 12 | `243 x 729` | 243 | `243 x 729` | 243 |
| 13 | `729 x 729` | 729 | `729 x 729` | 487 |
| 14 | `729 x 2,187` | 729 | `729 x 2,187` | 729 |

The `k=13` one-hot rank `487` is not anomalously low: because the three output
blocks sum to the all-ones matrix, `1+2*243=487` is the structural maximum at
that cut. These natural-digit matricizations therefore have maximal possible
rank in every row displayed. Exact low-bond-dimension tensor trains are ruled
out at these cuts.

There is also no visible recursive nesting between the four selected records.
For each of the three new most-significant digits, compare
`sigma_(k+1)(u+d 3^(k-2))` to `sigma_k(u)`. The nine agreement fractions lie
between `.330505` and `.334417`, while every conditional entropy
`H(sigma_(k+1) | sigma_k,d)` lies between `1.584743` and `1.584958` bits. In
this alignment the old policy predicts essentially nothing about the new one.
Because the records use different feasible parameters and are not canonical
critical eigenvectors, this is a finite diagnostic, not a theorem against an
all-level recursion.

## 5. Fresh strict and softened Perron comparisons

The selected-record conclusion is not just an artifact of storing rounded
certificates. A fresh float64 strict iteration at the `k=12` floating critical
estimate `lambda=1.8064236` reaches a Collatz--Wielandt log-width below
`10^-12`; its policy agrees with all `59,049` selected labels. The smallest
floating log-gap is `4.17e-7`. This agreement is numerical, not an interval
certificate, but it directly checks the scope mismatch noted above.

For the softened operator at `p=-1`, `lambda=1.9`, the winner labels of its
Perron vector agree with the selected hard policy on about `79.16%` of fibers
at `k=12` and `79.06%` at `k=13`. Smoothing changes a substantial minority of
labels, but it does not reveal a small winner circuit:

```text
k=12 soft winner aligned DAG nodes: 8,908 MSD / 7,930 LSD,
k=13 soft winner aligned DAG nodes: 22,252 MSD / 19,845 LSD.
```

Nor does the vector itself acquire a low-rank ternary tensor representation.
At relative Frobenius tolerance `10^-6`, the maximum QTT ranks are

```text
                 k=12    k=13
selected C         243      728
soft p=-1          243      728.                    (5.1)
```

Even at tolerance `10^-3`, the `k=13` ranks are `604` and `631`. These SVD
figures are floating diagnostics. They suggest that finite-temperature
smoothness is analytic smoothness of the gate, not low tensor complexity of
the global Perron vector.

The favorable fact is algorithmic: starting from the uniform vector, roughly
one hundred global layers recover the strict `k=12` and soft `k=12,13`
policies in this diagnostic. The separate spectral run from `k=8` through
`13` reaches a `10^-11` Collatz--Wielandt bracket for `p=-1` in
`61,67,74,80,87,94` iterations. This looks like `O(k)` depth over the finite
window. It remains an `O(k 3^k)` global computation, and no uniform
convergence-rate theorem currently turns it into a `poly(k)` coordinate
circuit.

## 6. Verdict and usable next target

There is a genuine **uniform circuit representation**, but presently only in
the output-linear/global sense. It can be useful for compact source-level
certificate generation: store `k`, the parameters, an iteration count, and a
rounding rule rather than a hand-written truth table. A verifier must still
evaluate exponentially many rows unless a new symbolic invariant is found.

There is no positive evidence for the stronger representation that would
matter to the endpoint proof:

```text
given k and the ternary digits of u,
compute sigma_k(u), or c_k(u), with poly(k) size/time. (6.1)
```

The exact OBDD, ANF, rank, dependency-cone, compression, and cross-level tests
all lean against the most natural versions of (6.1), while leaving general
circuits logically open. The most promising surviving formulation is not a
lossless policy circuit but an **approximate statistical circuit**: generate
coarse conditional laws or interval labels with a small tensor/automaton, and
prove that the unresolved near-tie set has sufficiently small selected mass.
That would dovetail with the soft-min susceptibility program. Exact generation
of every active label appears unnecessarily strong and is contradicted by the
finite complexity dashboard.

## 7. Mathematical inspiration

These comparisons were chosen for their representation theory, not because
the sources mention this number-theory problem.

* R. E. Bryant, [*Graph-Based Algorithms for Boolean Function
  Manipulation*](https://doi.org/10.1109/TC.1986.1676819), IEEE Transactions
  on Computers 35 (1986), is the canonical ordered-decision-diagram reference.
  The exact residual-function minimization above is its ternary analogue.
* I. V. Oseledets, [*Tensor-Train
  Decomposition*](https://doi.org/10.1137/090752286), SIAM J. Scientific
  Computing 33 (2011), motivates the matricization-rank and approximate-QTT
  tests.
* Balle, Panangaden, and Precup, [*A Canonical Form for Weighted Automata and
  Applications to Approximate Minimization*](https://arxiv.org/abs/1501.06841)
  (2015), supplies the neighboring Hankel-rank/weighted-automaton viewpoint.

The imported machinery diagnoses representation size. It does not provide a
new all-level policy theorem or a circuit lower bound beyond the explicitly
tested models.
